svg.text: Physical nodes, Docker containers, network segmentation, and security boundaries

g.text: 🔒 PII anonymization in ossec.conf • Encrypted transport AES-256

svg.text: «network» agent-net — Wazuh Agent Communication (Port 1514/TCP, AES-256 encrypted)

g.text: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS • 32GB RAM • 8 cores • Docker Compose deployment
🔒 Security: read-only containers • non-root • no-new-privileges • cap_drop ALL • resource limits
read_only: true • user: 1000 • cap_drop: ALL • mem: 2G • cpu: 2.0
🔒 Data volumes mounted read-only • DP ε=1.0 enforced
«network» internal-net (bridge, internal: true — no external access)
NOT in .env files — mounted read-only at /run/secrets/
Each org runs own Wazuh + AI Platform + FL Client
Connects to FL Server via encrypted gRPC
🔒 Only tree structures cross boundary — NO raw security data
(For FYP: simulated as Docker containers on same server)
